metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4869
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: mPins ( mouse PINS, aka. LGN, gene GPSM2) interacts with SAP102 (DLG3) and PSD95 (DLG4) in Y2H and pulldown assays.

Figure 5: Immunofluorescence imaging was used to investigate targeting of mPins (GPSM2) to dendritic spines. "mPins is enriched in dendritic spines where it colocalizes with native PSD-95 and SAP102"

Figure S1: Immunogold electron microscopy in the CA1 region of the hippocampus in P10 rat brain sections showed synaptic subcellular localization to postsynaptic densities, as well as apical dendrites (including some vescicular structures, authors suggest early endosomes). Furthermore, RER and Golgi localization was observed.. Experimental description: species:
authors refer to PMID: 11567040, which in turn refers to PMID: 11163274;
"Briefly, hippocampi were dissected from E18 Sprague-Dawley rats (Harlan) and dissociated in 0.25% trypsin."

antibody:
"mPins antibodies were characterized previously PMID:11781568"
"RNAi was carried out using siRNA as described PMID:11781568"

to disambiguate the target gene;
1) referenced paper PMID:11781568 that generated the antibody also used siRNA to target LGN(PINS homolog); "The sequence of the region targeted for siRNA in human LGN cDNA is GCTGCAGTTCAAGTTGGAACT"
2) nucleotide BLAST confirms GenBank NM_001321038 as top-hit, "G protein signaling modulator 2 (GPSM2)" with synonyms "LGN; PINS"

second antibody, denoted mPins420 in this paper, which was originally characterized in PMID:11832491 ;
"For LGN antisera, a peptide from human LGN (Ser420-Lys449) was synthesized and conjugated for generation of rabbit polyclonal antisera using the Peptide Synthesis and Antibody Production Facility at the Medical University of South Carolina. The antisera were affinity-purified prior to use for immunoblotting and immunocytochemistry as described previously"
